rulings.Case Title: DAVID E. SMITH SR. et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendants Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. and Kaiser Gypsum Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Mazda North American Operations¿ motion for summary judgment is denied. By not presenting contention interrogatory responses with its moving papers, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cronin was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. The evidence submitted with defendant¿s reply papers was not considered. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALFRED PISTILLI et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Borg-Warner Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Mr. Alfred Pistilli, Sr.¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether the decedent came into contact with products attributable to defendant. No other issue was fairly raised by defendant¿s separate statement. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp. successor by merger to Buffalo Pumps¿ motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Spirax Sarco, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Morgan was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets original to Sarco steam traps. Mr. Morgan¿s deposition testimony and plaintiffs¿ responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant did sustain its burden, Mr. Morgan¿s deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets original to Sarco steam traps. The only issue raised by the summary adjudication motion is punitive damages, which plaintiff says he has dismissed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIE HOWARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 16, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Atlas Welding Supply, Inc.¿s demurrer to plaintiff¿s complaint is sustained with 10 days leave to amend to give plaintiff an opportunity, if he is able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. = (220/HEK)
